data.description:
# Visual prompt + eval review surface (WS-VISUAL-EVAL)

## Context

Split out of the workflow-engine remediation epic `soupy-spool-revel` (6f2d) as an
explicit, separately-scoped decision+build. Today Langfuse (optional, when keys set)
gives a prompt-management + trace UI, and the `[eval]` extra wires Inspect/promptfoo
(each with their own viewers), but there is no first-class, documented surface for a
human to review prompts AND their eval STATUS together. The original epic framed this
as a candidate/decision workstream; this epic owns deciding it and, if approved,
building it.

## Why

Prompt edits are gated by evals (see 6f2d WS-EVAL-EXISTING), but reviewers/maintainers
have no single place to see "this prompt + its latest eval scores + kappa alignment +
recent traces" together. The decision is whether to make this first-class (and on what
substrate: Langfuse vs Inspect vs promptfoo vs a generated report) or to deliberately
keep the per-tool viewers and document that stance.

## Scope

- Decide: first-class surface vs keep-per-tool-viewers (ADR).
- If first-class: choose substrate; decide whether `a88f`'s "Langfuse = trace sink only"
  scoping should widen to prompt-management + eval status.
- Preserve the engine invariants (git-native prompts, clean diffs, schema-validated text).

## Success Criteria

- A recorded decision (ADR) with rationale and rejected alternatives.
- If build is chosen: a documented, reproducible surface a human can open to review
  prompts + eval status, wired behind the optional extras (no hard dependency).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ADR authored under `docs/adr/` recording the visual prompt+eval-review decision.
- [ ] Decision references the eval seam delivered by 6f2d WS-EVAL-EXISTING.
- [ ] If "build": the surface is implemented behind optional extras and documented in `docs/`.
- [ ] If "no build": the rationale for keeping per-tool viewers is documented and the WS is closed.
data.title: Visual prompt + eval review surface (WS-VISUAL-EVAL, split from 6f2d)
